briefing.opening: Welcome. We will now begin the climate briefing for July 2, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Widespread Impacts
sections.content: Globally, clear signs of a deepening climate crisis are evident. Last June, global sea surface temperatures reached an all-time high, and in Europe, the 'Omega heat dome' phenomenon led to new heatwave records, transforming an unprepared summer into a deadly one [10, 14, 17, 115, 125, 128, 129, 130, 133]. Such extreme heatwaves are directly impacting the agricultural sector, doubling economic damages for livestock farmers and causing changes in milk composition [16, 118, 123]. Furthermore, climate change is recognized as a widespread issue, not just a problem for polar bears, posing severe threats across entire ecosystems, including reshaping disease maps in India's mountainous regions and affecting even birds in Ontario's protected areas [4, 116, 121]. The surge in global livestock farming since 2006 has intensified pressure on nature, and the spread of invasive alien species and large-scale outbreaks of lovebugs amidst the climate crisis demonstrate the severity of ecosystem disruption [18, 135].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Climate Change Adaptation Efforts and Ecosystem Protection
sections.content: In response to the severity of climate change, various adaptation efforts are actively underway both domestically and internationally. Gangwon Fire Department participated in a policy conference to explore and present solutions and policies for large-scale forest fires in response to climate change, and the Korea SMEs and Startups Agency (KOSME) is also exploring solutions for large-scale forest fires in response to climate change at a policy conference [1, 2, 6, 34]. Domestic agricultural experts are discussing issues of climate change, food security, and agricultural digital transformation, while Wonju City is expanding the distribution of new potato varieties to cope with climate change [3, 11, 15]. The Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) is accelerating the use of standard scenarios, a 'common blueprint' for responding to the climate crisis, to establish a systematic adaptation foundation [9]. In Vietnam's Dong Thap region, efforts are being made to strengthen the climate change response capabilities of women entrepreneurs and promote eco-friendly production [13]. In Jeju, the restoration project for haenyeo (female divers') shelters, such as the 'Sureungkoji Bulteok' in Geumneung-ri which had collapsed due to climate change, is gaining momentum, regaining its former appearance, demonstrating a local community adaptation case [5, 113].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts and Challenges for Achieving Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Diverse domestic and international efforts are actively underway to achieve carbon neutrality goals. Suwon City has embarked on establishing an integrated carbon neutrality platform to systematically manage its goal of a 40% greenhouse gas reduction by 2030, while Haenam County and Yeosu City are operating mobile platforms to encourage citizens' participation in carbon neutrality practices [21, 32, 36, 41, 43, 67]. The Korea SMEs and Startups Agency (KOSME) is supporting carbon-neutral facility investments for 30 small and medium-sized enterprises and encouraging the participation of large and mid-sized companies, thereby responding to supply chain carbon regulations [20, 37, 47, 48, 54, 61, 62, 65, 66]. Gyeongsangbuk-do Province has been successively selected for national core technology competitions for carbon reduction and is investing approximately 220 billion KRW to leap forward as a next-generation carbon-neutral industrial hub. Gyeongsangnam-do Province is also transitioning to smart and carbon-neutral forest policies through its 6th Regional Forest Plan [28, 29, 30, 40, 50, 55, 64, 68, 69]. Meanwhile, concerns are also being raised that technological advancements could hinder the achievement of carbon neutrality, with reports indicating that greenhouse gas emissions from U.S. big tech companies are increasing due to the AI leadership competition, and gas power plants for U.S. data centers are expected to become a major source of climate change-related emissions [23, 49, 122].

sections.title: ⚡ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Policy Directions
sections.content: The grand energy transition towards renewable energy is accelerating both domestically and internationally, and the government is promoting the development of the next-generation direct current (DC) power grid industry to respond to the era of AI and renewable energy [74, 75, 84, 105, 111, 112]. SK and KKR have launched the largest integrated renewable energy corporation in Korea, planning to expand generation capacity to 10GW by 2031, and POSCO Group is also making renewable energy a core business axis [77, 79, 89, 96, 104, 108, 109]. Korea Southern Power (KOSPO) operates '24-hour Clinic,' a communication platform for renewable energy businesses, to address the difficulties faced by operators, while Korea Western Power (KOWEPO) is expanding its business with the goal of achieving 13.9GW of renewable energy by 2040, in line with the government's offshore wind power roadmap [70, 71, 72, 81, 83, 91, 95, 102, 107, 110]. Meanwhile, international transition efforts are also prominent, with China embarking on power system reforms to usher in an era where renewable energy accounts for 50% of power generation, and Scotland recording its highest-ever renewable energy generation [87, 88]. However, the potential for reduced income for small and medium-sized power generators and 'sunlight villages' due to the abolition of RPS (Renewable Portfolio Standard) and the slow progress of mandatory solar power policies for public parking lots suggest the need for policy supplements to expand renewable energy dissemination [76, 100, 106].

sections.title: ⚠️ Policy Challenges and Critical Perspectives in Climate Crisis Response
sections.content: In the process of responding to the climate crisis, policy challenges and critical perspectives on existing approaches are also gaining attention. At a discussion hosted by the National Assembly Climate Change Forum, criticism was raised regarding a 'supply-side omnipotence' approach to increasing future electricity demand, which aligns with the concern that mega-projects worth 1,800 trillion KRW could undermine climate crisis response efforts [7, 42]. Internationally, climate change is also identified as an intractable public policy issue, and policy pressure is intensifying, with Australia's Minister for Climate Change and Energy mentioning regulatory measures for energy companies [131, 132]. These discussions demonstrate the critical importance of social consensus and policy direction, beyond mere technological solutions, in addressing the climate crisis. New research findings also suggest that climate change strategies less reliant on carbon removal yield better outcomes [119].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights that amidst the deepening climate crisis, diverse adaptation and mitigation efforts are simultaneously underway both domestically and internationally. Amidst complex policy challenges and opportunities, we require an integrated and cautious approach towards a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Restoration of 'Bultuk,' Jeju Haenyeo's Resting Place, Gains Momentum
articles.original_title: 드라마에서 봤던 그 곳···제주 해녀들의 쉼터 ‘불턱’ 복원 ‘속도’
articles.description: The restoration of 'Bultuk,' a traditional resting place for Jeju Haenyeo, is gaining momentum. This year, the 'Sureungkoji Bultuk' in Geumneung-ri, Hallim-eup, was restored. To date, a total of 46 sites, including Haesindang, have been revived. These restored sites are planned to be integrated with Haenyeo Stay programs. The restoration efforts address structures that had collapsed due to recent coastal erosion and typhoons.

summary.phenomenon: The restoration project for 'Bultuk,' a traditional resting place for Jeju Haenyeo, is gaining momentum in Jeju Province. This year, the 'Sureungkoji Bultuk' in Geumneung-ri, Hallim-eup, was restored, and a total of 46 locations, including Haesindang, have been revived to date.
